mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends coincide with negative forward returns in more than 45% of periods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 consecutive months, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that triggers rapid risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE increases are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that compounds into sustained equity earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon only when both series rise together, not when either rises alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST acceleration episodes fail to produce SPX forward 60d returns above the unconditional mean at p > 0.10, or single-series triggers perform as well as the joint condition, negating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained rise in INDPRO reflects capacity utilization pressures that eventually compress margins and precede equity multiple contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the same INDPRO rise in early-cycle regimes instead signals healthy growth and positive returns, making the sign regime-dependent.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth > 3% while FEDFUNDS above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Late-cycle INDPRO acceleration episodes show positive forward 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the sign of the effect is indistinguishable from early-cycle episodes (interaction test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4% (annualized above roughly 5%), it signals an inflation surprise that raises expected future rate hikes, triggering a rapid equity sell-off over the following 5 trading days as markets reprice discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI surprises above threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth combined with a rise in UNRATE indicates a genuine labor market turning point rather than noise in either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; PAYEMS deceleration without a UNRATE rise (or vice versa) does not reliably predict returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain falls below 100k AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over same period
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS/UNRATE deterioration episodes fail to show significantly lower forward 20d SPX returns than the unconditional distribution (p > 0.10), or single-series triggers achieve comparable predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, GDPC1 growth reaccelerating above its trailing 4-quarter trend signals durable demand recovery that supports sustained equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in a mature expansion regime the same reacceleration instead signals overheating risk and produces no reliable directional edge.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds trailing 4-quarter average by > 1pp
failure_condition: GDPC1 reacceleration episodes in post-shock recovery regimes fail to produce forward 60d SPX returns exceeding the unconditional mean at p > 0.10, or the effect is equally strong in mature expansion regimes, invalidating regime-conditionality.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of high FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL growth signals a rising real policy rate that tightens financial conditions faster than nominal rates alone suggest, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; elevated FEDFUNDS alone without CPI deceleration does not produce the same effect because inflation offsets the real rate increase.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth falls below prior quarter's YoY growth
failure_condition: Joint high-FEDFUNDS/decelerating-CPI episodes fail to produce forward 20d SPX returns below the unconditional mean at p > 0.10, or high FEDFUNDS alone (without CPI deceleration) produces equally negative returns, negating the interaction claim.
